title: Yellow alert in state from tomorrow - The Times of India

full_text:
Trending
Kochi: India meteorological department (IMD) issued heavy rainfall with yellow alert for various districts from Sunday to Tuesday. Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Malappuram and Ernakulam are on yellow alert on Sunday.IMD said conditions are favorable for the southwest monsoon to advance in the next two to three days. Monsoon rain is expected to advance into more parts of southwest and southeast Arabian Sea, Lakshadweep area and most parts of Bay of Bengal.A cyclonic circulation persists over southeast Arabian Sea and its surrounding areas and is present over the southwest Bay of Bengal and adjoining Tamil Nadu coast. The cyclonic circulation persists over centraleast and southeast Bay of Bengal.Meanwhile, Alappuzha, Kollam and Kottayam districts received large excess rainfall in the past week between May 21 and 27, whereas Ernakulam recorded excess rainfall. However, Malappuram, Palakkad and Wayanad districts recorded large deficient rainfall in the past week and Kannur, Idukki and Kasaragod received deficient rains than normal. Remaining districts received normal rains.

title: Yellow alert in nine districts in Kerala; people should exercise caution till June 2, says IMD - Kerala Kaumudi
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Kerala
full_text:
THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: Kerala is likely to receive heavy rainfall today, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing a yellow alert in nine districts. The alert has been declared in Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Ernakulam, Idukki, Malappuram and Wayanad districts. No alerts have been issued for Saturday. However, a yellow alert has been declared for Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Ernakulam and Malappuram districts on Sunday.
The weather department has warned of isolated heavy rainfall in Kerala and Mahe today, Sunday, and on June 1 and 2. The IMD also said there is a possibility of widespread light to moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorms and strong winds.
